detail.title: SKILL.md Body, References, and TDD-for-Docs Validation

consumes.artifact: skills/arc-writing-skills/SKILL.md (methodology source — TDD Iron Law, frontmatter rules, word-count tiers)

produces.artifact: skills/arc-evaluating/SKILL.md updated for v2 routing/decision logic, body ≤ 1800 words (Comprehensive tier)
skills/arc-evaluating/references/preflight.md (new) — ceiling threshold, hash mechanics, PASS/BLOCK semantics
skills/arc-evaluating/references/verdict-policy.md (new) — INSUFFICIENT_DATA boundaries, k≥5 rationale, preflight exemption
skills/arc-evaluating/references/audit-workflow.md (new) — claim promotion and assertion retirement arbitration
Pressure-scenario artifacts (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR evidence) archived under skills/arc-evaluating-workspace/ per arc-writing-skills conventions

requirement.title: SKILL.md Description Stays Triggering-Only
requirement.description: The v2 SKILL.md frontmatter description field MUST describe triggering conditions only (start with "Use when...", third-person, specific symptoms). It MUST NOT summarize the v2 workflow (preflight, analyzer, audit, INSUFFICIENT_DATA, blind supplementary). Summarizing workflow into the description creates a discovery shortcut where Claude follows the summary and skips reading the full skill body — explicitly warned against by skills/arc-writing-skills/SKILL.md. Total frontmatter MUST remain ≤ 1024 characters.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: The updated description MUST start with "Use when" and MUST NOT contain verbs describing internal steps (run preflight, invoke analyzer, aggregate audit, emit verdict). It lists triggering conditions / symptoms only. fr-sb-001
The YAML frontmatter total length (name + description combined) MUST be ≤ 1024 characters measured as raw bytes of the YAML block. fr-sb-001
The frontmatter MUST contain exactly two fields: name and description. Any additional field (version, tags, model, agent, etc.) MUST be absent. fr-sb-001

requirement.title: SKILL.md Body Word Budget
requirement.description: The v2 SKILL.md body (content after the YAML frontmatter, excluding code fences) MUST NOT exceed 1800 words — the Comprehensive tier limit from skills/arc-writing-skills. The current v1 body is 2082 words; v2 adds preflight, verdict policy, claim-extraction, and blind supplementary concepts on top. Net size MUST be controlled by moving detail into references/. Any content that is (a) detailed schemas, (b) extended examples, (c) API tables, or (d) reference material applicable to multiple skills MUST move to references/ rather than inflating the body.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: `wc -w skills/arc-evaluating/SKILL.md` after v2 edits MUST return a number ≤ 1800 (body + headings, excluding frontmatter). fr-sb-002

Given the v2 body exceeds 1800 words during drafting, the author MUST extract content into references/ until the body is under budget. The body MUST NOT be shipped at Meta tier (> 1800) for a Discipline skill. fr-sb-002

requirement.title: Progressive Disclosure via New References Files
requirement.description: Three new reference files MUST be created and one existing file MUST be extended, so the v2 body stays lean while the detail remains discoverable on-demand. New files: references/preflight.md (ceiling threshold, hash mechanics, PASS/BLOCK semantics, how preflight interacts with fr-vr-001 exemption), references/verdict-policy.md (verdict enum including INSUFFICIENT_DATA, k thresholds, asymmetric delta thresholds), and references/audit-workflow.md (claim promotion and retirement arbitration procedure). Existing file references/grading-and-execution.md MUST be extended with the discovered_claims[] and weak_assertions[] schema documentation. SKILL.md MUST point at these files with clear loading guidance (e.g., "See references/preflight.md for ceiling-threshold mechanics.").

acceptance_criteria.criterion: skills/arc-evaluating/references/preflight.md, verdict-policy.md, audit-workflow.md MUST exist. Each MUST be substantive (≥ 300 words) and self-contained. fr-sb-003

skills/arc-evaluating/SKILL.md MUST reference each new file at least once with loading guidance phrased to help the agent know WHEN to read it (e.g., "when blocking on preflight ceiling", not just "see X"). fr-sb-003

The existing references/grading-and-execution.md MUST contain schema documentation for both discovered_claims[] and weak_assertions[] arrays, matching the contract in fr-gr-003 and fr-gr-004. fr-sb-003

requirement.title: Rationalization Table Updated for v2 Behaviors
requirement.description: SKILL.md MUST include a Rationalization Table listing v2-specific excuses and their counters, following the pattern from skills/arc-writing-skills (discipline-skill bulletproofing). The table MUST at minimum cover the following excuses: "this change is too small to eval", "time pressure, ship now eval later", "preflight blocks, just skip it once", "k=4 is close enough to 5", "INSUFFICIENT_DATA is advisory, I'll ship anyway", "the grader raised weak_assertions but the pass rate is fine".

acceptance_criteria.criterion: SKILL.md MUST contain a markdown table with columns "Excuse" and "Reality" (or equivalent) listing at least six v2-specific rationalizations with explicit counters. fr-sb-004
Each counter text MUST close the loophole (state what the agent MUST do), not merely restate the Iron Law abstractly. fr-sb-004

requirement.title: Red Flags List Updated for v2 Failure Modes
requirement.description: SKILL.md MUST include a Red Flags list — thoughts that, when detected, signal an imminent eval discipline failure. The list MUST cover v2-specific failure modes at minimum: "I already manually tested, eval is redundant", "this is docs-only, no eval needed", "the INSUFFICIENT_DATA banner is just a warning", "I can promote the discovered claim on my own without audit", "the blind comparator disagreed but assertions passed so it's fine", "preflight is new, I'll skip it this time and backfill later".

acceptance_criteria.criterion: SKILL.md MUST contain a "Red Flags" section (or equivalent) with a bullet list enumerating at least six v2-specific failure thoughts. fr-sb-005
The section MUST include a closing directive that every listed thought means: stop, re-read the skill, do not proceed. fr-sb-005

requirement.title: TDD-for-Docs Iron Law on v2 Edits
requirement.description: Per skills/arc-writing-skills Iron Law, the Iron Law "NO SKILL WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST" applies to EDITS of existing skills, not only new skills. v2 SKILL.md edits MUST be preceded by pressure-scenario evidence (RED phase) — a baseline subagent, WITHOUT the v2 skill edits, fails to follow v2 discipline on realistic prompts (e.g., tries to ship on k=3, skips preflight, treats INSUFFICIENT_DATA as advisory). The v2 edits (GREEN phase) MUST address those observed rationalizations verbatim. A REFACTOR phase MUST close new loopholes discovered in post-edit runs. Evidence MUST be persisted under skills/arc-evaluating-workspace/ per arc-writing-skills conventions, NOT discarded or summarized away.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Before v2 SKILL.md is committed, a RED-phase pressure-scenario artifact MUST exist under skills/arc-evaluating-workspace/iteration-1/ documenting at least three baseline violations (skipping preflight, ignoring INSUFFICIENT_DATA, or equivalent) with verbatim subagent rationalizations. fr-sb-006

After v2 SKILL.md is committed, a GREEN-phase artifact MUST exist showing the same pressure scenarios re-run WITH the v2 skill, demonstrating that the previously-failing subagent now complies. fr-sb-006

Any new rationalization observed in GREEN-phase runs MUST produce an entry in the Rationalization Table (fr-sb-004), an entry in the Red Flags list (fr-sb-005), or an explicit skill-body counter before the spec is considered complete. fr-sb-006

requirement.title: Cross-References Use REQUIRED Markers, Never @-File Syntax
requirement.description: When SKILL.md or any references/*.md file refers to another arcforge skill or rule, the reference MUST use the markers "**REQUIRED BACKGROUND:**" or "**REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:**" (or read the other skill via Skill tool at runtime). The at-sign file syntax (@path/to/file) MUST NOT appear — it force-loads context immediately, defeating progressive disclosure.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: A grep of skills/arc-evaluating/**/*.md for the regex "@[a-zA-Z0-9/_-]+\.md" MUST return zero matches after v2 edits. fr-sb-007
Any v2 skill reference to another arcforge skill (for example, arc-writing-skills, arc-using, arc-tdd) MUST use the REQUIRED BACKGROUND or REQUIRED SUB-SKILL marker syntax. fr-sb-007

requirement.title: Existing pytest Validators Must Continue to Pass
requirement.description: tests/skills/test_skill_arc_evaluating.py and tests/skills/test_eval_agents_contract.py MUST continue to pass after v2 changes. Because fr-fs-001 moves agent files into the skill folder and fr-ag-001 renames eval-comparator, test_eval_agents_contract.py likely needs path and name updates. Those updates are in scope; making the tests pass by deletion or disablement is forbidden.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After all v2 edits, `npm run test:skills` MUST exit 0 with the updated test files passing. fr-sb-008

No existing assertion in test_skill_arc_evaluating.py or test_eval_agents_contract.py MAY be removed or skipped to pass CI. If an assertion no longer applies, it MUST be rewritten to cover the v2 equivalent behavior, not deleted outright. fr-sb-008
